ogTitle: Eurunderee vs Evans Head: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Eurunderee and Evans Head.
heading: Eurunderee vs Evans Head — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Eurunderee, New South Wales and Evans Head, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Eurunderee has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Evans Head (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Eurunderee or Evans Head safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Eurunderee has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Evans Head scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Eurunderee scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Evans Head scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Eurunderee or Evans Head?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Eurunderee has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
